title: Shoreline
tagline: Full-stack dietary and kitchen operations control board for long-term care facilities.
description: Shoreline is a browser-based operations platform built for Ross Manor Care Home. Its primary module, iMPAC, manages resident diet orders, weekly menu planning, production worksheets, staff scheduling, supplement tracking, inventory, maintenance work orders, and administrative records — all from a single LAN-accessible control board with real-time multi-device sync via Server-Sent Events. Currently stable at v4.0.0; a v5 rebuild targeting React, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL is in progress.
story: Built after watching dietary staff juggle paper binders, spreadsheets, and disconnected tools to manage resident meal service at a care home. Shoreline starts with the host stand equivalent for dietary — the production worksheet and tray ticket workflow — and expands outward to cover every operational layer the kitchen and administration team touches daily.
journey: Shoreline grew from a single frustration: dietary operations in long-term care are operationally complex but chronically underserved by software. The v4 system was built to be immediately deployable on any facility LAN with zero database setup — just Node.js, Express, and file-based JSON storage. Every device on the network — dining room tablet, kitchen laptop, nurse's station — shares live data through SSE-pushed updates. The v5 rebuild is converting that proven feature set into a productized, multi-tenant platform with PostgreSQL, Prisma, and a modern React frontend.
forWho: Dietary managers, kitchen staff, and administrators at long-term care homes and care facilities who need a unified, real-time operations platform without the complexity or cost of enterprise healthcare software.

detailedSpecs.architecture: v4 (production): Node.js + Express REST API serving a vanilla JavaScript SPA with tab-based routing. File-based JSON storage — no database required. v5 (rebuild): React 18 + TypeScript + Vite frontend, Zustand state management, Express TypeScript backend migrating to PostgreSQL via Prisma, with Next.js SSR planned for Phase 3.
detailedSpecs.dataFlow: Client action (diet order, menu save, task update) → Express REST endpoint → JSON file write → SSE broadcast to /api/events → all connected clients refresh active view in real time. Offline fallback: api-layer.js switches to localStorage reads/writes when server is unreachable.
keyPatterns: Server-Sent Events (SSE) for real-time multi-device sync across LAN
Resident diet registry with allergen cross-contact audit against weekly menu planner
Production worksheet and tray ticket generation from live diet orders
Role-based frontend access (Admin, Supervisor, Cook, Server, Nurse, Note Taker)
File-based JSON storage with zero-dependency setup for LAN deployment
Offline/localStorage fallback mode when server is unreachable
Railway cloud deployment with persistent volume for data/ directory
detailedSpecs.deployTarget: LAN (any Windows/Mac/Linux machine running Node.js) or Railway (cloud, with persistent /app/data volume). Optional PM2 for persistent background process.
